transcript.clean_text: thank you well if anything is predictable in this Welter it was that we would have a breakaway on stage 11. 163 kilometers destination Laguna Negra the Black Lake well coming as it did after the time trial where Filippo Garner had gone extremely deep and blown the field away this well would there be wind no would there be high temperatures equally no would there be a breakaway absolutely nailed on oh GC man of course have some very high ground still to come what would be the advantage of chasing down a break on such Terrain well the pace was one thing that would be definitive this day it was extremely high as we started our run with just one climb at the very last to be contended albeit with ramps of around 14 and 16 so the race was on to get in the Breakaway and the jostling for positions had an impact sadly on the deck one ayuzo cut Jersey new bike off you go get on with it and they did Pace at the beginning of the day never really falling below 47 kilometers per hour it was high 26 Riders made it up the road including Garrett Thomas and Philip ogana who won the time trial the day prior really mama was up there Roma Gregoire he's a Serrata Luis Leon Sanchez Andy KRON stage winner here plenty of big names that really could invoke some pace and a challenge to each other though not overall best place man get around Thomas so it was all down to our finale the Gap was nudging up to around about the six minutes Mark a few had rolled the dice Paul ozala thought he would try for total energies who would set out to fill the Gap well they kind of stayed together driven on by philippo Garner who of course was working for Garrett Thomas an infernal Pace on some really testy ramps all rolls of the dice came to naught it seems when Ghana was in this kind of mood and it was destructive 26 rapidly started to filter down and we had some real favorites out front ing to hold on were some very credible Riders like Dorian godown but Ghana well he just kept the pressure up EF education easy posts well they went for it Jonathan kaithebo tried once and then tried again at the flam Rouge and in fact it looked like it would stick for the Ecuadorian away he went who would fill the Gap that was the question and this time it was Garrett Thomas that picked it up while staying with him was heza sarada and indeed Roman Gregoire as well from grupama Francis this year they had ideas of catching kathable and in fact they did it was to be a Spanish Victory first of the race for Jesus herrada and his third win on La Vuelta brilliant at work in fact waiting for him was his family dad included arms in the left in the air crossed the line with 14 16 ramps he'd done it super work and a great day's racing of course we had two races on the day with the GC of course still to come home well out of the group of favorites went Sean otterbrooks who perhaps was thinking about his position and indeed the possibilities of a top 10 finish for this race but really it was all down to others around him oh Dbacks had gone so Hugh carthy had to protect his position overall as well and we were looking back down the mountain and of course Remco having a pop wearing The Young Riders leaders jersey he went for it set because went with him robblich likewise effectively all neutralized each other harada taking it ahead of Gregoire and cron on the day and as far as the GC is concerned virtually no change Spain gets its winner and the race starts to ponder what is still to come it's amazing how the pain of a day could dissipate with victory harada taking it by a margin of three seconds ahead of Gregoire Andy cron cotebo go right Thomas doing himself all kinds of favors overall he'll be starting to think about targeting a top 10 place here job well done honors even I think amongst the favorites sepsis holds his margin in the red jersey but of course with the tourmallay and many other big climbing days to come for how much longer will The Americans lead this race so they're in second place Evan Paul roguelitch Lenny Martinez your top five with Almeida after a good time trial still in a good position polka dot Jersey transfers to the shoulders of Jesus herrada courtesy of his victory today tomorrow no climbing points on offer at all so harada will stay in the polka dots guaranteed it is however going to be a big day for the sprinters plenty of points up for grabs Kaden Groves will hope to hold on to that and indeed gain another Victory perhaps his third of the race let's see how it pans out shall we we'll see you tomorrow foreign
